docs.title: ⚙️ Python Automation Scripts — Advanced Tutorials
docs.text: A comprehensive collection of production-grade automation scripts covering file systems, web scraping, browser automation, Excel/CSV processing, email, scheduling, APIs, and more.

docs.title: 🗂️ 1. File & Folder Automation
docs.text: Automate repetitive file management tasks — renaming, organizing, deduplication, and backups.

docs.title: 🌐 2. Web Scraping
docs.text: Extract structured data from any website — static pages, paginated results, and JSON APIs.

docs.title: 🖥️ 3. Browser Automation (Selenium / Playwright)
docs.text: Automate real browser interactions — logins, form fills, screenshots, and scraping JS-heavy sites.

docs.title: 📊 4. Excel & CSV Automation
docs.text: Generate reports, clean messy data, merge sheets, and build ETL pipelines with Pandas and openpyxl.

docs.title: 📧 5. Email Automation
docs.text: Send plain text, HTML emails, and bulk campaigns with attachments via SMTP.

docs.title: ⏰ 6. Task Scheduling
docs.text: Schedule Python scripts to run at specific times — daily reports, hourly backups, cron-style jobs.

docs.title: 🔌 7. API Automation
docs.text:
Automate REST API workflows — GitHub, Sheets, Slack, webhooks, and custom APIs.

Example — GitHub API: Auto-create Issue:

import requests

TOKEN = "your_github_token"
REPO  = "ashishbindra2/automation"

def create_issue(title, body, labels=None):
    url = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{REPO}/issues"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"token {TOKEN}"}
    data = {"title": title, "body": body, "labels": labels or []}
    r = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
    print(f"Issue created: {r.json()['html_url']}")

create_issue("Bug: Script fails on Windows", "Traceback...", ["bug"])

Example — Kill process by name:

import psutil

def kill_process(name):
    for proc in psutil.process_iter(["name", "pid"]):
        if proc.info["name"] == name:
            proc.kill()
            print(f"Killed {name} (PID {proc.info['pid']})")

kill_process("chrome.exe")

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the root:

EMAIL_USER=your@gmail.com
EMAIL_PASS=your_app_password
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx

Load in any script with:

from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os
load_dotenv()
EMAIL_USER = os.getenv("EMAIL_USER")

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing
docs.text:
Pull requests are welcome! For major changes, open an issue first.

  1. Fork the repo
  2. Create your feature branch: git checkout -b feature/new-script
  3. Commit: git commit -m "Add: new automation script"
  4. Push: git push origin feature/new-script
  5. Open a Pull Request
Made with ❤️ by [Ashish Bindra](https://github.com/ashishbindra2) · [LinkedIn](https://in.linkedin.com/in/ashishbindra2) · [Twitter](https://twitter.com/bindra_ashish) ⭐ **Star this repo** if it helped you!

docs.title: To install library
docs.text: pip install python-pptx-1.0.2

docs.title: Empty ppt file
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation

prs = Presentation() 
prs.save('./ppt_file/eg.pptx')

docs.title: 1. slide_layouts
docs.text: A presentation can have more than one slide master and each master will have its own set of layouts. This property is a convenience for the common case where the presentation has only a single slide master.

docs.title: To add the specfic layout
docs.text:
lys = prs.slide_layouts[0]
lys = prs.slide_layouts[1]
lys = prs.slide_layouts[2]

docs.title: To dipslay all the layouts
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation

prs = Presentation()
lys = prs.slide_layouts[0]

for _ in lys:
    prs.slides.add_slide(_)

prs.save('./ppt_file/eg.pptx')

docs.title: Add title to slide
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation

prs = Presentation()

first = prs.slide_layouts[0]
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(first)

prs.save('./ppt_file/eg2.pptx')

docs.title: Hot to add title in slide
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation

prs = Presentation()

first = prs.slide_layouts[0]
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(first)

title = slide.shapes.title
title.text = "Hello world!!! 🐱‍🚀"
prs.save('./ppt_file/eg3.pptx')

these boxes are placeholders

docs.title: placeholders eg
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation

prs = Presentation()

first = prs.slide_layouts[0]
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(first)

# set the title
title = slide.shapes.title
title.text = "Hello world!!! 🐱‍🚀"

# the place holders
subtitle  = slide.placeholders[1]
subtitle.text = "sub"
prs.save('./ppt_file/eg4.pptx')

docs.title: list of layout names
docs.text:
for slide in prs.slide_layouts:
    print(slide.name)
prs.save('./ppt_file/eg5.pptx')

Title Slide
Title and Content
Section Header
Two Content
Comparison
Title Only
Blank
Content with Caption
Picture with Caption
Title and Vertical Text
Vertical Title and Text

total 11 layouts are there

docs.title: lets add bullets point
docs.text:
second = prs.slide_layouts[1]
slide_second = prs.slides.add_slide(second)

slide_second_title = slide_second.shapes.title
slide_second_title.text = "Going to add Bullets"

slide_second_body = slide_second.shapes.placeholders[1]

if slide_second_body.has_text_frame:
    tf = slide_second_body.text_frame
    tf.text = '23123213'

docs.title: add more Bullets points
docs.text:
slide_second_title = slide_second.shapes.title
slide_second_title.text = "Going to add Bullets"

slide_second_body = slide_second.shapes.placeholders[1]

tf = slide_second_body.text_frame
tf.text = '23123213'

p = tf.add_paragraph()
p.text = "subdata"

p1 = tf.add_paragraph()
p1.text = "3"

docs.title: add nested
docs.text:
tf = slide_second_body.text_frame
tf.text = '23123213'

p = tf.add_paragraph()

p.text = "subdata"
p.level = 1
p1 = tf.add_paragraph()

p1.text = "3"
p1.level = 2
prs.save('./ppt_file/eg7.pptx')

has_text_frame can be used to determine whether a shape can contain text. (All shapes subclass _BaseShape.) When_BaseShape.has_text_frame is True,

_BaseShape.text and_TextFrame.text are provided to accomplish the same thing

docs.title: add_textbox()
docs.text:
add_textbox

  • always require 4 paramters add_textbox( left: Length, top: Length, width: Length, height: Length ) -> Shape
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt

prs = Presentation()

# blank layout 7
blank_layout = prs.slide_layouts[6]
blank_slide = prs.slides.add_slide(blank_layout)

left = top = width = height = Inches(1)

txt_box = blank_slide.shapes.add_textbox(left,top,width,height)
txt_frm = txt_box.text_frame

txt_frm.text = "text box example"

docs.title: bold the text and incrase the size
docs.text:
p = txt_frm.add_paragraph()
p.text = "This is a second paragraph that's bold"
p.font.bold = True

p = txt_frm.add_paragraph()
p.text = "This is a third paragraph that's big"
p.font.size = Pt(40)

prs.save("./ppt_file/eg8.pptx")

Output:

docs.title: change the text box posistion
docs.text:
top = Inches(4)
left = width = height = Inches(1)

txt_box = blank_slide.shapes.add_textbox(left,top,width,height)
txt_frm = txt_box.text_frame
txt_frm.text = "text box example"

docs.title: Inches
docs.text:
• always require 1 arguments
class Inches(inches: float)
Convenience constructor for length in inches.

docs.title: add_picture()
docs.text:
Add picture shape displaying image in image_file.

image_file can be either a path to a file (a string) or a file-like object. The picture is positioned with its top-left corner at (top, left). If width and height are both |None|, the native size of the image is used

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt

prs = Presentation()

# blank layout 7
blank_layout = prs.slide_layouts[6]
blank_slide = prs.slides.add_slide(blank_layout)

img_path = "./input/Animals in Love.jfif"

left = top = Inches(1) 
# it take 4 argument left and top are compulsory only 
# with and height are not
pic1 = blank_slide.shapes.add_picture(image_file=img_path,left=left,top=top,height=Inches(3))
pic2 = blank_slide.shapes.add_picture(image_file=img_path,left=Inches(5),top=top,height=Inches(3))

prs.save("./ppt_file/eg9.pptx")

Output

docs.title: add_shape()
docs.text: 5th layout which is title only

docs.title: center the shape
docs.text:
left = Inches(0.93)  # 0.93" centers this overall set of shapes
top = Inches(3.0)
width = Inches(1.75)
height = Inches(1.0)

shape_Mso_sh = shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.PENTAGON, left, top, width, height)
prs.save("./ppt_file/eg10.pptx")

docs.title: all shape of MSO_SHAPE
docs.text:
# center the shape
left = Inches(0.93)  # 0.93" centers this overall set of shapes
top = Inches(3.0)
width = Inches(2.75)
height = Inches(2.0)

for shape in MSO_SHAPE:
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(title_layout)
    shapes = slide.shapes
    shapes.title.text = shape.name

    shape_Mso_sh = shapes.add_shape(shape, left, top, width, height)

prs.save(f"./ppt_file/MSO_SHAPE.pptx")

# center the shape

left = Inches(0.93)  # 0.93" centers this overall set of shapes
top = Inches(3.0)
width = Inches(1.75)
height = Inches(1.0)

shape = shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.PENTAGON, left, top, width, height)
shape.text = 'Step 1'

left = left + width - Inches(0.4)
width = Inches(2.0)  # chevrons need more width for visual balance

for n in range(2, 6):
    shape = shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.CHEVRON, left, top, width, height)
    shape.text = f'Step {n}'
    shape.text = 'Step %d' % n

    left = left + width - Inches(0.4)


prs.save(f"./ppt_file/eg11.pptx")

docs.title: add_table
docs.text:
The table has the specified number of rows and cols and the specified position and size. width is evenly distributed between the columns of the new table. Likewise, height is evenly distributed between the row

rows = cols = 2
left = top = Inches(2.0)
width = Inches(6.0)
height = Inches(0.8)

table = shapes.add_table(rows, cols, left, top, width, height).table
# set column widths
table.columns[0].width = Inches(2.0)
table.columns[1].width = Inches(4.0)

# write column headings
table.cell(0, 0).text = 'Foo'
table.cell(0, 1).text = 'Bar'

# write body cells
table.cell(1, 0).text = 'Baz'
table.cell(1, 1).text = 'Qux'

prs.save(f"./ppt_file/eg12.pptx")

docs.title: Extract all text from slides in presentation¶
docs.text:
• text_runs will be populated with a list of strings,
  • one for each text run in presentation
